project_title: Benchmarking Generative AI in EDA Workflows

research_scope.objective: Evaluate the capability and reliability of open-source generative models to automatically generate synthesizable Verilog HDL for standard digital circuit design tasks.

dataset.description: Task library of standardized digital circuit specifications and corresponding verified HDL + testbenches.

example.specification_text: Design a 4-bit ripple carry adder in Verilog. Inputs: a[3:0], b[3:0]; Outputs: sum[3:0], carry_out.

prompt_templates.example_prompt: Design a 4-bit ripple carry adder in Verilog.

prompt_templates.description: Specification + I/O interface definition (used in Phase 2+)
prompt_templates.example_prompt: Write synthesizable Verilog for a 4-bit synchronous up-counter with enable and reset. Module interface: input clk, reset, en; output reg [3:0] count.
prompt_templates.note: Each phase runner uses a single fixed prompt style. Cross-template sensitivity comparison (A vs B vs C) has not been implemented.

prompt_templates.description: Specification + sample I/O examples (not yet used in any benchmark run)
prompt_templates.example_prompt: Design a 4-bit binary to gray code converter. Input-output examples: 0000→0000, 0001→0001, 0010→0011, 0011→0010. Write synthesizable Verilog.
prompt_templates.note: Template C is defined here for reference. No benchmark run has used or compared this template.

stages.name: Model Inference
actions: Feed each specification prompt to all three models.
Collect HDL outputs with metadata (model name, seed, runtime).

stages.name: Functional Simulation
procedure: Pair generated DUT with reference testbench.
Compile with iverilog and run with vvp.
Parse PASS/FAIL output from testbench stdout.

pipeline_stages_not_implemented.note: The following pipeline stages are defined here as design goals but have NOT been built in any benchmark run.

synthesis_analysis.description: Yosys-based logic synthesis to extract cell count, logic depth, and area proxy.
synthesis_analysis.reason_not_implemented: The SynthesisTool class exists in Eval_Pipeline.py but is never called by any phase runner (run_phase1 through run_phase5). All phase runners construct EvaluationMetrics manually with gate_count=None, cell_count=None, estimated_area=None.

testbench_effectiveness.description: Fault injection pipeline to measure testbench detection rate.
testbench_effectiveness.reason_not_implemented: No fault injector has been built. fault_detection_ratio is always None in all benchmark results.

implemented.name: Syntax Validity
implemented.definition: Percentage of Verilog files that compile without syntax errors.

implemented.name: Functional Correctness
implemented.definition: Percentage of designs that pass all test cases in the reference testbench simulation.

implemented.name: Generation Time
implemented.definition: Time (seconds) for model inference per task.

implemented.name: Iteration Count
implemented.definition: Number of generation-evaluation cycles before the best result was accepted. Phase 4+ only.

implemented.name: Confidence Entropy
implemented.definition: Entropy over token log-probabilities from the model; lower entropy = higher confidence. Phase 4+ only.

implemented.name: Waveform Diff Summary
implemented.definition: Summary of signal mismatches between generated and reference waveform. Phase 4+ when enabled.

implemented.name: Formal Equivalence Status
implemented.definition: Result of formal equivalence check between generated DUT and reference. Phase 4+ when enabled.

implemented.name: Semantic Repair Applied
implemented.definition: List of semantic repair operations applied during iterative refinement. Phase 4+ only.

not_implemented.note: The following metrics were originally specified as goals but have NOT been computed in any benchmark run.

synthesis_quality.definition: Average cell count and logic depth of synthesized design.
synthesis_quality.reason: Requires Yosys synthesis; SynthesisTool class exists in code but is never invoked by any phase runner. gate_count, cell_count, and estimated_area are always None in all results.

testbench_detection_rate.definition: Fraction of injected faults detected by generated testbench.
testbench_detection_rate.reason: Fault injection pipeline was never built. fault_detection_ratio is always None.

prompt_sensitivity.definition: Variance in FC across different prompt templates (A/B/C) for the same model.
prompt_sensitivity.reason: Each phase runner uses a single fixed prompt style. Cross-template comparison has not been run.

hallucination_index.definition: Average count of undeclared signals or impossible constructs per 100 lines of HDL.
hallucination_index.reason: No code exists to count undeclared signals. This metric has never been computed.

usability_score.reason: Requires SQ and TDR which are not implemented. This formula has never been evaluated.

quantitative_analysis: Aggregate per-model averages and standard deviations for SV and FC.
Run paired statistical tests (Wilcoxon/t-test) for FC and SV between models.
Compute confidence intervals (95%) for functional correctness.
qualitative_analysis: Inspect representative failed outputs for syntax, interface, or logic errors.
Classify failures: syntax, interface mismatch, behavioral bug, non-synthesizable construct.
Present examples and categorize root causes.
visualizations: Bar chart: Functional pass rate per model per task category.

tools_and_environment.automation: Python orchestration scripts (run_phase1.py through run_phase5.py) automate model querying, compilation, simulation, and metric logging.

environment.containerization: Docker image with EDA + Python dependencies

deliverables: A reproducible open-source benchmark suite for HDL generation.
Quantitative comparison of syntax validity and functional correctness across small, medium, and large open models.
Error taxonomy highlighting failure types and their frequencies.
Insights into model scaling, post-processing strategies, and iterative refinement.
expected_outcomes.contribution: Establishes a structured, reproducible benchmark for generative HDL generation, providing measurable baselines for future EDA-AI research.
